name: Ray Barretto
description: American percussionist and bandleader of Puerto Rican ancestry (1929–2006)
abstract: Raymundo "Ray" Barretto Pagán (April 29, 1929 – February 17, 2006) was an American percussionist and bandleader of Puerto Rican descent. Throughout his career as a percussionist, he played a wide variety of Latin music styles, as well as Latin jazz.

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Donaldson
about_quotes.evidence: Then I got Ray Barretto to steady down the rhythm... You were one of the first jazz group leaders to use a conga player, as you were describing with Ray Barretto on those Blue Note dates from 1957.

about_quotes.speaker: Paquito D'Rivera
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions Ray Barretto only in response to the interviewer's question about a conversation the interviewer had with Barretto; the interviewee does not describe any personal interaction or relationship with Barretto.
about_quotes.doc_title: Paquito D'Rivera — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
